**Dina huvudsakliga ansvarsområden:**
* Leda och koordinera Major Incident-processen end-to-end
* Säkerställa snabb och korrekt eskalering till rätt tekniska resurser
* Driva kommunikation till intressenter, både operativa team och ledning
* Följa upp incidenter med fokus på rotorsaksanalys (RCA) och förbättringsåtgärder
* Säkerställa efterlevnad av ITIL-processer och interna styrmodeller
* Samverka med externa leverantörer och säkerställa att SLA:er uppfylls
* Bidra till kontinuerliga förbättringar inom incidenthantering och beredskap
* Delta i beredskap/standby enligt överenskommen rotationsmodell


 Profil
   

  

 Vi söker dig som är en trygg ledare i kritiska situationer och som trivs med att arbeta i högt tempo där tydlig kommunikation och beslutsförmåga är avgörande.
   

  

**För att lyckas i rollen ser vi att du:**
* Har en stark förmåga att leda utan formellt personalansvar
* Är strukturerad, stresstålig och lösningsorienterad
* Har mycket god kommunikativ förmåga och kan anpassa budskap till olika målgrupper
* Har förmåga att skapa förtroende och samverkan mellan olika team och leverantörer
* Är proaktiv och drivande i förbättringsarbete
* Har ett helhetsperspektiv på IT och förstår beroenden mellan olika system och tjänster


**Kravprofil/Meriter**
* Erfarenhet av Incident Management eller Major Incident Management i större IT-miljö
* God kunskap om ITIL (Incident, Problem och Change Management)
* Erfarenhet av att arbeta i miljöer med flera leverantörer (outsourcing/sourcing-modeller)
* Erfarenhet av att hantera affärskritiska incidenter med hög påverkan
* Vana att kommunicera med ledning och stakeholders under tidspress
* Erfarenhet av rotorsaksanalyser och förbättringsarbete
* Certifieringar inom ITIL eller liknande ramverk är meriterande
* Erfarenhet från retail eller annan verksamhetskritisk drift är ett plus

title: Staff Design Engineer, EU
description:
Hi 👋🏾 I’m Abhik, Ashby's Co-Founder and VP of Engineering. This role is close to my heart because, as someone who can both design and code, it’s where I’ve always done my best work, but also where I was seen as a rebel and an outsider. I want folks like me to feel at home at Ashby, and so I made Design Engineering a formal role and department that works closely with me. Our first hire was over five years ago, and we’re doubling the team from five to over ten in the next year.
   

  

 This role truly expects you to design and code. Design Engineer at Ashby isn’t just a Frontend Engineer with new branding, nor is it a Designer vibe coding prototypes. Combining excellence in both is where magic happens. I found that when I put my best effort into both the design and technical implementation of a feature, I had a nimbleness and creativity that was hard to achieve when I did only one or the other. For instance, the UX and UI I envisioned often influenced the data model's design and flexibility, while the understanding of technology's capabilities often simplified or improved the design. This role embraces that.
   

  

 You’ll Work On Our Most Challenging Design Problems, Help Others Improve Their Designs By Expanding And Enhancing Our In-house Design System, And Consult On Bespoke Design Work Needed By Product Engineers. To Ground It With Examples, Design Engineers At Ashby Have:
   

  

* Redesigned our mobile web app by talking with customers who use it often, wireframing new flows, implementing its design system, and using that system to turn the wireframes into a polished UI.
* Built a set of flexible, composable components in our design system that allow other engineers to build beautiful, consistent setup wizards across our product easily.
* Helped a Product Engineer improve the information hierarchy and scannability of their design for viewing a candidate’s assessments. Recruiters can quickly parse information and pick out anomalies.


 The Design Engineer Role Is More Common Today Than Five Years Ago, But I Believe Ashby Offers a Unique Opportunity That Few Can Match:
   

  

* You work on a product at scale, not at an early-stage startup struggling to find users and get feedback on what you’ve designed and built. At Ashby, your work will touch over 100,000 weekly active users, millions of candidates per week, and notable customers like Notion, Linear, Shopify, and Snowflake. You’ll get to test out ideas with our own recruiting team and hiring managers who use Ashby every day (like me), and often hear customer feedback as early as the day you release.
* This role has always had the commitment of both Benji (CEO & Co-Founder) and me: I’ve held the role, steadfastly championed it since we started hiring in 2020, and haven’t diluted its responsibilities as we’ve grown (in fact, we’ve doubled down).
* You get to shape the UI and UX of a powerful and complex product. Our users need software that helps them move quickly while adapting to their unique workflows and preferences. As a result, our UI isn’t a simple chatbox, but it can’t rely on a sea of knobs and dials either. It needs to feel approachable to someone using it for the first time while offering increasingly powerful ways to customize and automate their work.


**Why You Shouldn’t Apply**
 Design Engineers Come In Many Flavors, Not All Of Which Fit Our Model. Here Are Some Reasons You Might Not Enjoy The Role:
   

  

* You only want to work on design systems. While improving our design system is one of many responsibilities, you won’t be able to work on it exclusively.
* You like to do extensive research and user testing before implementation. The beauty of being part-Engineer is that you can build conviction by shipping to a subset of users (including our own team) and gathering feedback!
* You want everything to be perfect before it gets into a user’s hands. One of the drivers of our success is that we ship fast. While we appreciate the strive for perfection, we also realize it doesn’t need to be achieved all at once; instead, we iterate over time, often letting user feedback and business needs drive prioritization.
* You don’t have excellent taste and execution in visual design. Design Engineers set the bar for visual design in our app and continually improve it, pushing its boundaries with each new feature or redesign.
* You need company-driven process and structure to get your projects across the finish line. Sprint planning and well-defined project management processes are things you need or look to others to lead. You’d rather focus on the design and technical details.
* You only want to do exciting work. We’re building a team of kind, collaborative folks. Customer issues and investigations are distributed across the team, including our high-level ICs.


****What Seniority/Level To Apply For****
 We’ve posted levels from Junior to Staff. The higher the level, the more experience and alignment with the role we expect when reviewing your application and while interviewing.
 *Please apply to the one that sets the right expectations.*
* Junior Design Engineer - You should have no more than 2 years of industry experience as a designer or engineer. We want to see projects (personal or professional) with at least a couple of users that showcase great visual taste and burgeoning talent in both UI/UX and Engineering.
* Design Engineer - This posting covers both Mid and Senior levels. You have 2+ years of continuous experience (e.g., internships don’t count) as an Engineer or a Designer. You have good proficiency in both Design and Engineering, with exceptional proficiency in the discipline you practice full-time. Regardless of which discipline you’re coming from, we expect experience designing products and shipping code to hundreds of users (even if through side projects).
* Staff Design Engineer (This Posting) - We’re looking for folks who’ve practiced our flavor of Design Engineering professionally. It may not be through a formal title, but you’ve made major contributions to a design system and designed and implemented features for hundreds of users and iterated on them through user feedback.


 Internally, we do not use these titles, but Engineers are leveled based on proficiency (which you can read about here).
   

  

 What We’re Building
   

  

 As engineers, we are used to tooling that makes us
 **better** 
 at what we do. When we started Ashby, we saw the opposite with Talent Acquisition software. Recruiting teams were leveling up how they did their work, but instead of software meeting this new standard, it held them back.
   

  

 Scheduling a final round is an excellent example. Recruiting teams wanted to schedule candidates faster, track interviewer preparation and quality, and do it with half the headcount. A recruiter needed to manually collect availability from the candidate, identify qualified interviewers, perform “Calendar Tetris” to find who is available to interview the candidate, schedule on the earliest date possible, and make any last-minute adjustments as availability changed. They must do this while considering the interview load on each individual and whether interviewers need to be trained and shadowing others. 🥵 TA software didn’t help.
   

  

 As hiring managers, we know TA is a critical function, and as engineers, we know software can do better. So, we built and continue to build Ashby to give TA teams the
 *highest* 
 standard of tooling. Software that’s intelligent and powerful. Software that provides insights into where they’re failing and automates or simplifies many of the tasks they’re underwater with. We want other functions and departments to be jealous of what TA teams can do with Ashby, and today they often are!
   

  

 Engineering Culture
   

  

 Our Engineering Culture Is Motivated By Benji’s (my Co-founder And CEO) And My Belief That a Small, Talented Team, Given The Right Environment, Can Build High-quality Software Fast (and Work Regular Hours!). We Do It Through:
   

  

* Minimal process with ownership over decisions normally made by product and design
* Natural collaboration and deliberate communication
* Investing in tools and abstractions that give us leverage
* Putting effort into building a diverse team


**Minimal Process & Lots of Ownership**
 The best engineers we’ve worked with delivered reliably magical outcomes. They took customer problems and relentlessly drove them to solutions that were not only successful but often brilliant and creative. While they did this with minimal oversight, stakeholders were never in the dark as to what was going on, and no setback was a surprise.
   

  

 Traditional product-development processes aren’t meant for the best engineers. Their purpose is to create consistent outcomes regardless of the engineer’s skill. But, consistency comes at the expense of an engineer’s time and freedom—both ingredients necessary to generate those magical outcomes. As a result, process stifles the best engineers and doesn’t give others the opportunity to practice the behaviors that made the best engineers the “best.”
   

  

 At Ashby, we want to build an environment that encourages every engineer to be their best. So, at Ashby, every Engineer runs their project. Product Managers (and Designers) build strategy, do customer research, and hand off problem briefs to Engineers. Engineers take on the rest: they research the problem, write product specs, build wireframes, and implement their solution end-to-end. We rely on engineers, not process, to push information outward to the relevant folks (e.g., Product Managers) and pull folks in to help (e.g., Designers, Infra). It’s a new level of ownership for many engineers, but we’d rather an engineer fail a bit and coach up their skills than use process as a crutch. Not everyone succeeds in our culture, but those who do
 *thrive* 
 .
   

  

**Collaboration is Natural & Communication is Deliberate**
 Our engineering team consists of lifelong learners who are talented but also humble and kind (meet them here!). These attributes create an environment where collaboration happens naturally. We combine this with research, prototyping, and written proposals to see around corners and get feedback from the team across time zones. Focus time is something that we hold sacred, and, with thoughtful and deliberate communication, engineers are in <2h meetings per week (I wrote about it here).
   

  

 To Drive It Home, Here's a Recent Calendar Of An Engineer Who Has Been With Us For Over 4 Years. ~34 Hours Of Focus Time, 2.5h Of Interviews, And 3.5 Hours Of Meetings:
   

  

 We also meet in person at least twice a year, once as a department and once as a company. You also have a small budget to meet up with folks in your city/region.
   

  

**Increase Leverage, not Team Size**
 We Built Ashby With The Quality, Breadth, And Depth That Many Customers Would Expect From Much Larger Teams Over Larger Time Scales. We’ve Done This Through Investment In:
   

  

* Great developer tooling. Our CI/CD takes ~10m, and we deploy at least 15x a day. A debugger that works out of the box. Everyone on the team has contributed to our developer experience 💪🏾.
* Building blocks to create powerful and customizable products fast. At the core of Ashby is a set of common components (analytics modeling and query language, policy engine, workflow engine, design system) that we constantly improve. Each improvement to a common component cascades throughout our app (short video below).
* AI-powered tooling. We think of AI as a way to automate the mundane parts of building and maintaining high-quality software. We use a combination of third-party and internally built tools that, for instance, auto-triage customer issues, suggest fixes, prototype ideas, generate production-ready code, and conduct code reviews. Engineers have an unlimited token budget (but are not measured on it). We write in detail about our philosophy, current use of AI, and future plans for AI in Engineering here.


**And a Demo Of One Of These Building Blocks:**
 Here’s an impromptu quote from Arjun in our company Slack of what it’s like to build a feature at Ashby:
   

  

**Put Effort into Diversity**
 Diverse teams drive innovation and better outcomes. Having seen my mother and partner build their careers as minority women in non-diverse fields, I want to make sure Ashby creates opportunities for the next generation of engineers from underrepresented groups.
   

  

 Today, 25% of engineers and 50% of our engineering leaders at Ashby are from underrepresented groups. We are taking conscious steps to improve, like sourcing diverse candidates, providing generous paid family leave, no leetcode interviews, and more.
   

  

**Interview Process**
 At Ashby, our team and interview process want to help you show your best self. We’ll dive into past projects and simulate working together through pair programming, designing, writing design system specs collaboratively, and discussing decisions. There are no leetcode or whiteboard exercises.
   

  

**Our Interview Process Is Three Rounds:**
* Introduction call with me (30m, live). Be prepared to screen-share examples of your work.
* A second round where we either do a technical screen (1h, live) or a design take-home (~3h async, 30m live)
* Three interviews, a deep dive into a past design system or design project, a design system interview, and the interview we didn’t do in the second round: technical screen or design take-home. (3h, live)


 Depending on our leadership team’s bandwidth, we may start with an additional 30m screen with a recruiter.
   

  

 I will be your main point of contact and prep you for interviews. Each round will have written guidance so you know what to expect. You’ll meet 3-4 people in Design Engineering (with 5-15 minutes in each interview to ask them questions). If we don’t give an offer, we’ll provide feedback!
   

  

**Your First Three Months at Ashby**
 We want an exceptional onboarding experience for every new hire. At Ashby, your dev environment is set up with a single script, you push your first product change on day one, and you spend the rest of your time shipping product changes that give you a tour of our codebase and best practices. The product changes increase in scope and ambiguity from simple copy changes to the delivery of a prominent, impactful feature. Your manager will do a 30, 60, and 90-day review to give feedback and calibrate on how we work together.
   

  

 It’s a team effort to get you successfully onboarded; you’ll have a peer paired with you to answer questions, pair program, and check in often to see if you need help. The rest of the team will run training sessions on our culture, product, engineering process, and technical architecture.
   

  

**Technology Stack**
 Our tech stack is TypeScript (frontend & backend), React, GraphQL API, Node.js, Postgres, and Redis. Depending on the level you’re applying for and your past experience, we expect, at a minimum, good proficiency with TypeScript, React, and CSS. Proficiency with backend technologies is a plus, as it allows you to work without coordinating with Product Engineers.
   

  

****Benefits****
* Competitive salary and equity.
* 10-year exercise window for stock options. You shouldn’t feel pressure to purchase stock options if you leave Ashby —do it when you feel financially comfortable.
* Unlimited PTO, and we will encourage you to take it.
* A minimum of 12 weeks of fully paid parental leave, covered by Ashby. For folks outside the US, it may be longer to be in line with regional requirements.
* Generous equipment, software, and office furniture budget. Get what you need to be happy and productive!
* $100/month education budget with more expensive items (like conferences) covered with manager approval.
* If you’re in the US, we offer top-tier health insurance for you and your dependents, with 100% of premiums covered by Ashby. In other countries, we provide high-quality supplemental health insurance for you and your dependents, also fully covered by us.


 Ashby’s success hinges on hiring great people and creating an environment where we can be happy, feel challenged, and do our best work. We’re being deliberate about building that environment from the ground up. I hope that excites you enough to apply.
   

  

*Ashby provides equal employment opportunities (EEO) to all employees and applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, genetics, sexual orientation, gender identity, or gender expression. We are committed to a diverse and inclusive workforce and welcome people from all backgrounds, experiences, perspectives, and abilities.*
*Ashby is committed to a fair and transparent hiring process. We confirm that this advertisement is for an active, existing vacancy within our organization. Please be advised that we may use artificial intelligence-driven tools to assist our recruitment team in screening, assessing, and selecting candidates for this position.*
 Compensation Range: €141K - €226K

title: Head of Support
description:
*Software Point – A LabVantage Company in the Nordics* 




**Vill du leda en supportorganisation i framkant av nordisk hälso- och sjukvårds-IT? Software Point söker en driven och erfaren Head of Support som vill ta ägarskap över kundupplevelsen och lyfta supporten till nästa nivå.** 




  





**Om Software Point** 




 Software Point är ett ledande nordiskt bolag inom digitalisering av hälso- och sjukvård, specialiserat på laboratorieinformationssystem (LIS) och IT-lösningar för verksamhetskritiska miljöer. Som en del av LabVantage Solutions-koncernen arbetar vi med kunder som ställer de högsta kraven på kvalitet, tillgänglighet och professionalism – sjukhus, laboratorier och regioner i hela Norden.
 



  





 Hos oss möter du en internationell, innovativ miljö med engagerade kollegor och kunder i centrum. Vi är ett bolag i tillväxt och erbjuder en roll med verklig påverkan.
 



  





**Om rollen** 




 Som Head of Support leder du och ansvarar för Software Points supportorganisation och är en av bolagets viktigaste kontaktytor mot kund. Du spelar en central roll i att bygga långsiktiga kundrelationer, skapa förtroende och säkerställa en hög kundupplevelse i alla lägen.
 



 Rollen innebär nära samverkan med kundansvariga, programansvariga, projektledare och utvecklingsteamet. Du är den person som håller ihop supportleveransen, driver förbättring och säkerställer att teamet och kunderna alltid har vad de behöver.
 



  





 Tjänsten kan placeras vid något av Software Points kontor i Danderyd, Linköping och Lund. Resor inom Sverige och Norden ingår i rollen.
 



  





**Dina ansvarsområden** 



* Leda och utveckla supportorganisationen och dess medarbetare.
* Ansvara för hög kvalitet, tillgänglighet och professionalism i kundkommunikation och support.
* Agera eskaleringsinstans och problemlösare i komplexa kundsituationer.
* Samverka tätt med sälj, projekt och utveckling för sömlös kundleverans.
* Driva kontinuerlig förbättring av supportprocesser, rutiner och verktyg.
* Följa upp KPI:er och kundnöjdhet och rapportera till ledning.
* Representera supportorganisationen i strategiska kunddialoger.



  





**Vi söker dig som** 



* Har djup teknisk förståelse och erfarenhet av komplexa IT-miljöer.
* Har erfarenhet av kundnära arbete inom hälso- och sjukvård, regionmiljö eller annan verksamhetskritisk IT – gärna i avancerad laboratoriemiljö.
* Har erfarenhet av att effektivisera support processer och erfarenhet av att sätta upp och mäta KPI:er för att säkerställa kundnöjdhet och kvalitet.
* Dokumenterad erfarenhet av case management system/service management system (Jira o dyl.)
* Är trygg i kommunikation med tekniska specialister, verksamhet och ledning på alla nivåer.
* Har hög organisationsförmåga och kan hantera flera parallella aktiviteter och kunddialoger.
* Ser support som en kvalificerad och strategisk tjänst och drivs av att skapa värde för kunden.
* Är lösningsorienterad, ansvarstagande och trivs i en modern och snabbföränderlig miljö.
* Kommunicerar obehindrat på svenska och engelska. Övriga nordiska språk är ett plus.



  





**Vi erbjuder** 




 En central och strategisk ledarroll i ett bolag i tillväxt.
 



 Möjligheten att forma och bygga supportorganisationen och sätta din prägel på kundupplevelsen.
 



 En internationell arbetsmiljö med kunder och kollegor i hela Norden.
 



  





 Arbete i framkant av digitalisering inom hälso- och sjukvård; ett område med stor samhällsbetydelse.
 



  





 Flexibel placering vid valfritt kontor i Sverige.
 



  





**Ansökan** 




 I den här rekryteringen samarbetar Software Point med Just Right AB. Vi arbetar med löpande urval och ser gärna att du skickar in din ansökan snarast.
 



  





 Vid frågor, välkommen att kontakta rekryteringskonsult Catarina Isaksson, catarina.isaksson@justright.se, 072-205 45 48 eller
 



 Anna-Karin Engvall, annakarin.engvall@justright.se, 0707-323007
 



  





**Välkommen med din ansökan så snart som möjligt. Intervjuer kommer att ske löpande.** 




  





*Software Point – A LabVantage Company in the Nordics | www.softwarepoint.com*

title: Information Security Specialist
description:
**Om organisationen** 




 Hotell- och Restauranganställdas a-kassa (HRAK) hanterar tryggheten för 87.000 medlemmar inom hotell-, restaurang- och besöksnäringen. Med ett viktigt samhällsuppdrag bidrar HRAK varje dag till ekonomisk trygghet för människor över hela landet.
 



  





 Organisationen befinner sig i en spännande utvecklingsfas där digitalisering, informationshantering och säkerhet blir allt viktigare områden. För att möta ökade krav från omvärlden, myndigheter och medlemmar satsar HRAK nu vidare på sitt arbete inom informationssäkerhet och söker därför en Informationssäkerhetssamordnare som vill ta ett helhetsansvar för att utveckla och driva det systematiska informationssäkerhetsarbetet framåt.
 



 Du blir en del av IT- och utvecklingsverksamheten och arbetar nära verksamhet, ledning, IT-konsulter och externa leverantörer. Rollen är nyinrättad, vilket innebär en unik möjlighet att vara med och bygga upp, forma och utveckla arbetssätt på området. Här får du en central roll med stort inflytande och möjlighet att göra verklig skillnad i en organisation där informationssäkerhet är en strategiskt viktig fråga.
 



  





 HRAK erbjuder en verksamhet med korta beslutsvägar, engagerade kollegor och ett tydligt samhällsuppdrag där ditt arbete bidrar till både verksamhetsutveckling och ökad trygghet för medlemmarna. Kontoret ligger centralt på Kungsholmen och har en unik och modern design.
 



  





  





**Om rollen** 




 Som Informationssäkerhetssamordnare ansvarar du för att samordna, utveckla och följa upp HRAKs systematiska informationssäkerhetsarbete. Du blir motorn i organisationens ledningssystem för informationssäkerhet (LIS) och arbetar både strategiskt och operativt för att säkerställa att verksamhetens information hanteras på ett säkert och regelrätt sätt.
 



  





 En viktig del av uppdraget blir att etablera och vidareutveckla organisationens ledningssystem för informationssäkerhet tillsammans med verksamhetens ledning. Du fungerar som en naturlig kontaktpunkt mellan verksamhet, IT, externa leverantörer och myndigheter.
 



  





***Exempel på arbetsuppgifter:***




 • Bygga upp, förvalta och vidareutveckla ledningssystem för informationssäkerhet (LIS)
 



 • Utveckla styrdokument, policys, riktlinjer och ramverk inom informationssäkerhetsområdet
 



 • Genomföra risk- och sårbarhetsanalyser samt konsekvensbedömningar
 



 • Hantera och följa upp informationssäkerhetsincidenter
 



 • Säkerställa efterlevnad av regelverk, interna policys och säkerhetskrav
 



 • Utbilda och öka säkerhetsmedvetandet hos medarbetare och chefer
 



 • Vara rådgivande stöd till verksamheten, ledning och IT-funktion
 



 • Delta i verksamhetsprojekt med informationssäkerhet som särskilt fokusområde
 



 • Ställa säkerhetskrav på och följa upp externa IT-leverantörer
 



 • Representera HRAK i externa forum och kontakter med bland annat Sveriges A-kassors Säkerhetsråd, IMY, Myndigheten för civilt försvar samt externa samarbetspartners
 



 • Rapportera informationssäkerhetsläget till ledning och beslutsfattare
 



  





  





**Vem är du?** 




 Vi söker dig som kombinerar ett starkt säkerhetstänk med ett pragmatiskt och verksamhetsnära arbetssätt. Du trivs i en roll där du får ta initiativ, driva frågor framåt och skapa struktur. Samtidigt har du förmågan att kommunicera komplexa säkerhetsfrågor på ett pedagogiskt sätt och skapa förståelse hos olika målgrupper.
 



 Du är självgående, analytisk och trygg i att arbeta både strategiskt och operativt. Eftersom rollen innebär många kontaktytor ser vi även att du är kommunikativ, samarbetsorienterad och har god förmåga att skapa förtroende i organisationen.
 



  





**Våra skallkrav:** 




 • Eftergymnasial utbildning inom exempelvis systemvetenskap, datavetenskap, informationssäkerhet, cybersäkerhet eller liknande område
 



 • Cirka 3 års arbetslivserfarenhet inom informationssäkerhet eller närliggande
 



 • Kunskap inom GDPR och informationshantering
 



 • Erfarenhet av riskanalyser, styrdokument och säkerhetsrelaterade processer
 



 • God teknisk förståelse och erfarenhet från IT-miljöer eller IT-verksamhet
 



 • Erfarenhet av att driva, samordna eller följa upp säkerhetsrelaterade initiativ och projekt
 



 • Mycket goda kunskaper i svenska och engelska, i tal och skrift
 



  





**Meriterande:** 




 • Erfarenhet av att bygga upp eller förvalta ledningssystem för informationssäkerhet (LIS)
 



 • Kunskap om ISO 27001, NIS2 och andra relevanta ramverk
 



 • Certifieringar såsom CISM, CISSP eller CISA
 



 • Erfarenhet av arbete inom offentlig sektor eller annan reglerad verksamhet
 



  





  





**HRAK erbjuder** 




 På HRAK får du möjlighet att kombinera ett viktigt samhällsuppdrag med ett kvalificerat specialistområde där du får stort mandat att påverka.
 



  





***Du erbjuds:***



* En nyckelroll med stort ansvar och hög synlighet i organisationen
* Möjlighet att bygga upp och utveckla informationssäkerhetsarbetet från grunden
* En verksamhet med korta beslutsvägar och engagerade kollegor
* Kompetensutveckling inom ett område som blir allt viktigare
* En stabil organisation med ett tydligt samhällsuppdrag
* Goda möjligheter att påverka arbetssätt, processer och framtida utveckling
* Grundlig introduktion och goda förmåner



  





  





**Övrig information** 




 Anställningsform: Tillsvidareanställning, med inledande provanställning
 



 Start: Enligt överenskommelse
 



 Omfattning: Heltid, måndag till fredag
 



 Plats: Stockholm
 



  





 Den här rekryteringsprocessen hanteras av A-hub, och mail angående tjänsten går genom A-hub. Ansök genom att klicka på "Ansök" nedan. Tänk på att vi lägger stor vikt vid din motivation och intresse för tjänsten.
 



  





  





**Om HRAK** 




 Hotell- och Restauranganställdas a-kassa (HRAK) erbjuder ekonomisk trygghet till medlemmar inom hotell-, restaurang- och besöksnäringen. Med ett tydligt samhällsuppdrag, hög servicegrad och fokus på utveckling arbetar HRAK för att skapa trygghet och stöd genom hela arbetslivet.

title: Account Executive – Enterprise Growth & New Business (Nordics)
description:
Are you a high-performing, hunter-led sales professional with a passion for winning new enterprise business? Do you thrive in building new logo pipelines while also unlocking value in underdeveloped accounts?
 



  





 Colt is hiring an Account Executive covering the Nordics (Sweden, Denmark, Finland, Norway), based from our Stockholm office. This role is focused on driving new business acquisition (60–70%) alongside targeted account development (30–40%), making it ideal for a commercially driven sales professional who enjoys both hunting and strategic account growth.
 



  





**What You’ll Be Doing** 




  




* Drive new logo acquisition across large and mid-market enterprises in the Nordic region, targeting multiple verticals
* Build and execute a strong hunting strategy, identifying, engaging, and converting new opportunities into bookings (ARR)
* Develop and grow dormant or underpenetrated accounts, reactivating relationships and identifying expansion opportunities
* Lead full sales cycles from prospecting through to closing complex enterprise deals
* Build and maintain C-level and senior stakeholder relationships across target accounts
* Deliver upsell, cross-sell, renewals, and upgrades across Colt’s portfolio
* Leverage Colt’s solutions portfolio including:
* Connectivity (WAN, Ethernet, IP)
* Cloud connectivity (AWS, Azure, GCP)
* SD-WAN / SASE / security overlays
* Collaborate with pre-sales, product, and delivery teams to ensure successful deal execution



  





**What You Bring** 




  




* 5–10 years of enterprise B2B sales experience, with a strong emphasis on new business (hunter profile)
* Proven track record of:
* Winning new logos in large and mid-market enterprises
* Developing pipeline and closing complex deals
* Managing and expanding dormant or underperforming accounts
* Strong experience in one or more of:
* Network/connectivity solutions (WAN, Ethernet, IP)
* Cloud connectivity (AWS, Azure, GCP)
* SD-WAN, SASE, and security overlay solutions
* Background in industries such as:
* Telco / Digital Infrastructure
* Software / SaaS
* Cloud & Security
* Demonstrated ability to operate across multiple Nordic markets
* Strong commercial acumen and familiarity with sales methodologies (e.g. MEDDPICC, Challenger, SPIN)
* Self-starter mindset with a high level of accountability, drive, and resilience

title: Databricks Champion
description:
Choosing Capgemini means choosing a company where you will be empowered to shape your career in the way you’d like, where you’ll be supported and inspired by a collaborative community of colleagues around the world, and where you’ll be able to reimagine what’s possible. Join us and help the world’s leading organizations unlock the value of technology and build a more sustainable, more inclusive world.


**Your role**
  

  

We are now seeking a passionate Databricks Expert/Champion or someone to growing into that space to join us. In this role, you will play an important role in shaping and contributing to Capgemini’s data, analytics & AI strategy forward using the Databricks platform. You will work hands-on with the latest tools in data ingestion, ETL, orchestration, and machine learning, while also contributing to solution design, architecture, and customer engagement. This is a role for someone who thrives at the intersection of technology, strategy, and communication.
  

  

Capgemini has a strong and close collaboration with Databricks and was named EMEA Partner of the Year 2024 & 2025 – a recognition of our customer growth, joint engagements, and technical excellence. As part of our team, you’ll help strengthen this partnership and guide organizations in unlocking the full potential of the Databricks platform.
  

  

We know that great candidates don’t always meet every requirement. If you’re interested in the role, we encourage you to apply even if your experience doesn’t match every point listed.
  

  

*In this role you will play a key part in:*

* Design and implement high-performance data pipelines using Databricks and Apache Spark
* Build and maintain secure data lakehouses and data warehouses in Azure
* Integrate AI/ML models into data pipelines using Databricks ML and Azure ML
* Lead or contributing to solutioning and architecture efforts – from whiteboard to production
* Collaborate with cross-functional teams including data scientists, analysts, and DevOps engineers
* Participate in presales, technical workshops, and customer presentations
**Your profile**  

  

We see this role being a great fit for someone with experience as a data engineer or architect with a passion for building scalable, high-impact data solutions. You combine deep technical skills with strong communication and solutioning capabilities.
  

  

*You have:*


* Proven experience designing and implementing data platforms using Databricks, Apache Spark, SQL and Python
* Several years of hands-on experience with Microsoft Azure, including services like Azure Data Lake Storage, Azure Data Factory, and Azure ML
* A solid understanding of data architecture, including lakehouse design, data modeling and governance
* Experience working in presales, technical workshops and customer-facing presentations
* A collaborative mindset and the ability to translate business needs into technical solutions
  

  

**Databricks Champion – or on your way there**

  

*We are looking for someone who is either:*

* An **official Databricks Champion**, recognized for their technical excellence and community contributions, or
* A **high-level Databricks expert** with the ambition and potential to become a Champion. With Capgemini’s support, network, and close partnership with Databricks, we’ll help you take that next step.
  

  

**What we believe you will enjoy about working here**

* We recognize the significance of flexible work arrangements to provide support. Be it remote work, or flexible work hours, you will get an environment to maintain healthy work life balance.
* At the heart of our mission is your career growth. Our array of career growth programs and diverse professions are crafted to support you in exploring a world of opportunities.
* We partner with names such as Google, Microsoft, Amazon and Databricks, which can equip you with valuable certifications in the latest technologies in line with your ambitions.
  

  

**Application**

*As part of our recruitment process, identity verification will be conducted through inspection of your ID card, and background checks may be carried out as required. We are committed to building a diverse and inclusive workplace and welcome applications from people of all backgrounds, experiences and perspectives. We particularly encourage women and underrepresented groups in tech to apply.*


**About Capgemini**
  

Capgemini is an AI-powered global business and technology transformation partner, delivering tangible business value. We imagine the future of organizations and make it real with AI, technology and people. With our strong heritage of nearly 60 years, we are a responsible and diverse group of 420,000 team members in more than 50 countries. We deliver end-to-end services and solutions with our deep industry expertise and strong partner ecosystem, leveraging our capabilities across strategy, technology, design, engineering and business operations. The Group reported 2024 global revenues of €22.1 billion.
  

  

**Make it real** | www.capgemini.com
company_description: Capgemini is an AI-powered global business and technology transformation partner, delivering tangible business value.

title: Cloud Infrastructure Architect AWS
description:
Choosing Capgemini means choosing a company where you will be empowered to shape your career in the way you’d like, where you’ll be supported and inspired by a collaborative community of colleagues around the world, and where you’ll be able to reimagine what’s possible. Join us and help the world’s leading organizations unlock the value of technology and build a more sustainable, more inclusive world.


**Om rollen**  

  

Vill du vara med och forma framtidens molnlösningar?
  

  

Du blir en del av Capgeminis Cloud & Infrastructure Services (CIS)-enhet med fokus på projekt- och konsulttjänster i Norden. Vårt mål är att erbjuda kunder helhetslösningar inom Cloud och vi erbjuder en mängd olika tjänster till våra kunder, från strategisk rådgivning till projektgenomförande.
  

  

*I denna roll kommer du ha en nyckelroll i att:*
  

* Designa och implementera komplexa, skalbara och säkra AWS-lösningar anpassade efter kundens behov
* Ge teknisk vägledning och support vid felsökning under en hel projektleverans
* Samarbeta med intressenter för att samla in krav och föreslå effektiva migrationsstrategier
* Agera som en betrodd rådgivare till kunder gällande branschtrender och ny teknik
* Dela kunskap inom organisationen genom mentorskap, utbildning och skapande av återanvändbara artefakter

**Din Profil**
  

* Minst 7 års erfarenhet av praktisk design av molnbaserad företagsarkitektur, lagrings- och nätverkslösningar
* Minst 5 års erfarenhet av att bygga, migrera och modernisera företagsapplikationer på enterprisenivå
* Minst 5 års erfarenhet av molntransformation
* AWS-certifieringar på professionell nivå (t.ex. Solutions Architect Professional, DevOps Engineer Professional) samt erfarenhet av automatisering och skriptning (t.ex. Terraform, Python, CDK, shell, Ruby, Powershell)
* Kunskap om säkerhets- och efterlevnadsstandarder (t.ex. HIPAA, GDPR)
* Flytande i Svenska och engelska, både i tal och skrift

  

Önskvärda kvalifikationer:
  

* Erfarenhet av AWS-tjänster (t.ex. AWS Application Migration Service (MGN), Cloud Migration Factory, Portfolio analysis tooling) samt hantering av storskaliga migreringar från datacenter till datacenter och/eller från datacenter till molnet; drift av datacenter (t.ex. datasäkring, övervakning, larm och notifieringar)

* Erfarenhet av att integrera AWS AI/ML-tjänster (SageMaker, Bedrock, Amazon Q); förståelse för styrning inom AI/ML och ansvarsfulla AI-praktiker


**Vad vi tror kommer få dig att trivas:**
  

* Konkurrenskraftig lön, förmånliga tjänstepensioner och kollektivavtal som ger trygghet
* Fokus på din hälsa och ditt välmående, med initiativ för att skapa balans mellan jobb och fritid
* En arbetsmiljö med kollegor som stöttar och inspirerar varandra, och chefer som har ditt bästa i fokus
* Brett utbud av karriärmöjligheter och tillgång till Capgeminis stora utbildningskatalog, särskilt inom den senaste teknologin för t.ex. cloud och generativ AI
* En företagskultur där mångfald och inkludering värdesätts högt

**Ansökan**
  

*I denna rekrytering hanterar vi ansökningar löpande. Som en del av vår rekryteringsprocess kommer identitetskontroll att genomföras genom granskning av ditt ID-kort, och bakgrundskontroller kan utföras vid behov. Vid frågor om tjänsten är du välkommen att kontakta ansvarig rekryterare Jessica Fogelsjöö, Jessica.fogelsjoo@capgemini.com.*


Till denna roll söker vi medarbetare för säkerhetsklassat uppdrag placerad hos kund.


*#LI-JF1*


Capgemini is a global business and technology transformation partner, helping organizations to accelerate their dual transition to a digital and sustainable world, while creating tangible impact for enterprises and society. It is a responsible and diverse group of 340,000 team members in more than 50 countries. With its strong over 55-year heritage, Capgemini is trusted by its clients to unlock the value of technology to address the entire breadth of their business needs. It delivers end-to-end services and solutions leveraging strengths from strategy and design to engineering, all fueled by its market leading capabilities in AI, generative AI, cloud and data, combined with its deep industry expertise and partner ecosystem.

title: SAP Test Manager
description:
Är du redo för nästa steg i din SAP-karriär? Vill du arbeta i en flexibel miljö med spännande uppdrag? Vi söker nu en SAP Project Manager som vill vara med och forma framtidens lösningar. Välkommen till CGI

**Dina framtida arbetsuppgifter**


Som SAP-testchef kommer du att spela en nyckelroll i våra leveranser till kunder inom olika branscher. Ditt ansvar kommer att inkludera:  

* Leda och hantera end-to-end SAP-testaktiviteter inklusive:
* Teststrategi
* Testplanering
* Testutförande
* Defekthantering
* Testrapportering
* Definiera QA-styrprocesser, teststandarder och bästa praxis.
* Samordna mellan affärs-, funktionella, utvecklings- och infrastrukturområden.
* Hantera SIT-, UAT-, regression-, integrations- och releasetestcykler.
* Säkerställ att testleveranser uppfyller kvalitets-, tids- och efterlevnadskrav.
* Driv SAP-testautomationsinitiativ
* Bygg och underhåll återanvändbara automationsramverk för SAP-applikationer.
* Integrera automation i CI/CD-pipelines där det är tillämpligt
**Kvalifikationer för att bli framgångsrik denna roll**


Krav på kvalifikationer för att lyckas i denna roll  

* 5+ års erfarenhet av ledarskaps-/hanteringsroller inom SAP-testning.
* Stark erfarenhet av SAP-testning inom SAP ECC eller S/4HANA-miljöer.
* Praktisk erfarenhet av testhantering och felspårningsverktyg.
* Erfarenhet av att hantera storskaliga integrations- och regressionstestprogram.
* Stark förståelse för QA-metodiker och SDLC/STLC.
* Stark erfarenhet av automatiseringsverktyg, Tricentis Tosca föredras.
* Erfarenhet av SAP-automationsramverk och modellbaserad testning.
* Exponering för:

  

SAP Solution Manager  

Jira / Azure DevOps / ALM  

CI/CD-integration  

API- och gränssnittstestning  

  

* Erfarenhet av SAP Activate-metodiken föredras

  

#LI-CGISWEDEN5**Låt oss som ägare omvandla verklig insikt till handling – tillsammans.**  

  

Livet på CGI bygger på ägande, lagarbete, respekt och tillhörighet. Tillsammans med oss kommer du att kunna nå din fulla potential.  

  

Du är inbjuden att ta ägandeskap redan från första dagen i vårt arbete med att förverkliga vår dröm om att skapa en arbetsmiljö där vi trivs med att arbeta tillsammans och bidrar till framgången för vårt företag. Det är därför vi kallar oss CGI Partners snarare än anställda. Vi drar nytta av vår kollektiva framgång och tillsammans får vi möjlighet att aktivt forma vår företagsstrategi och inriktning.  

  

Ditt arbete skapar värde. Du kommer att vara med att utveckla innovativa lösningar och bygga relationer med medarbetare och kunder samtidigt som du får tillgång till globala möjligheter att skala upp dina idéer, omfamna nya möjligheter och dra nytta av gedigen bransch- och teknikexpertis.  

  

Du kommer att forma din karriär tillsammans med ett företag som är byggt för att växa och bestå. Du får stöd av ledare som bryr sig om din hälsa och ditt välbefinnande och ger dig möjligheter att fördjupa dina kunskaper och vidga dina vyer.
  

  

Bli en del en av världens största leverantörer av IT- och affärskonsulttjänster.

title: SAP Test Manager
description:

Come join our team—one of the largest IT and business consulting services firms in the world.

title: Automation Engineer (Information Security)
description:
**About Gelato**
----------------


Gelato has created the world’s largest global network for on-demand production of custom products – from t-shirts and mugs to books and wall art. We empower a new generation of ecommerce entrepreneurs to share their creativity worldwide while embracing local, sustainable production. By producing locally and perfectly matching supply with demand, we eliminate waste and reduce carbon emissions.


At Gelato, we don’t own production facilities – we build the software that connects them. With over 140 production partners in 32 countries, our network can deliver custom products to five billion people in just 72 hours. It’s smarter, faster, and greener.


With GelatoConnect, our SaaS platform, we’re driving innovation in print production. GelatoConnect simplifies operations for print producers by automating procurement, workflows, and logistics into one seamless system. By boosting efficiency and cutting costs, it helps businesses scale to meet market demands. With GelatoConnect, we’re reshaping the print industry to be more efficient, sustainable, and profitable.

**Platform**
------------


Gelato is an AI-first, cloud-native platform transforming local, on-demand production and delivery — with zero physical assets. We power:

* **Global production orchestration:** connecting e-commerce customers to 130+ partners across 32 countries. From AI-guided design to smart routing and logistics, we deliver to over 5 billion people in under 72 hours — faster, greener, smarter.
* **GelatoConnect:** our AI-driven print production OS that helps producers master digital printing — from inventory to shipping — by automating every step: procurement, prepress, printing, packaging, and dispatch.

**AI at the core**
------------------


We are not just early adopters of AI — we’re AI-native builders. Security at Gelato is no different: we don’t just use AI tools, we build them into everything we do.

* AI-first security operations using LLMs for alert triage, threat summarisation, runbook drafting, and incident documentation.
* Autonomous AI agents driving detection, alerting, and response orchestration across our security tooling.
* A culture of continuous experimentation — we actively encourage using AI to go further, faster, without cutting corners on rigour.

**The position**
----------------


As Gelato continues its global expansion, we are seeking an Automation Engineer to join our growing security function. This is a hands-on, builder-focused role centred on designing, building, and shipping the AI and automation that multiplies what our security team can do. Rather than triaging alerts by hand, you’ll build the systems that triage them.


Working closely with the Head of Information Security and the wider security team, you’ll turn manual, repetitive security work into reliable automated workflows and intelligent agents. You’ll integrate our security tooling, wire up LLM-driven pipelines, and create the automation that keeps the business safe at scale.


You don’t need to have built everything before. You need strong engineering instincts, genuine curiosity, and the drive to ship. You’ll have a clear path to grow your skills across security engineering, AI/agent development, and detection-and-response automation.


Our security team operates as enablers, not blockers — your job is to build the tooling that keeps the business safe without slowing it down.

**Responsibilities**
--------------------

### **Security automation engineering**

* Design, build, and maintain automation that removes manual toil across detection, triage, response, and reporting.
* Integrate security tooling (SIEM, EDR, IAM, cloud, ticketing) into seamless, reliable automated workflows.
* Build and maintain SOAR-style playbooks and pipelines for enrichment, containment, and response.
* Own the deployment, monitoring, and reliability of the automation you ship.

### **AI and agent development**

* Build LLM-powered tools and autonomous agents for alert triage, threat summarisation, runbook generation, and incident documentation.
* Develop and refine prompts, guardrails, and evaluations to make AI workflows safe and dependable in production.
* Prototype, test, and ship AI-assisted approaches to threat hunting, log analysis, and security operations.

### **Tooling and integrations**

* Develop integrations and APIs that connect security tools to internal systems and data sources.
* Write clean, maintainable, well-tested code.
* Maintain and improve internal libraries, documentation, and shared automation infrastructure.

### **Collaboration and enablement**

* Partner with the security team to identify the highest-leverage automation opportunities.
* Document workflows clearly so the team can operate, trust, and extend what you build.
* Automate evidence collection and control testing for compliance frameworks including ISO 27001, SOC 2, and NIST CSF.

**Requirements**
----------------

* 2–4 years of experience in software engineering, automation/DevOps, security engineering, or a related hands-on building role — including internships, apprenticeships, or equivalent practical exposure.
* Strong scripting and programming skills, particularly in Python, with a track record of building integrations against APIs.
* Experience building automation workflows — whether through SOAR platforms, CI/CD, or workflow orchestration tools (e.g. n8n, Tines, Zapier).
* AI-native working style — practical, hands-on experience building with LLMs (APIs, prompt engineering, and ideally agents), not just using them as a chat assistant.
* Comfortable working in cloud environments, particularly AWS — you understand IAM, logging, and serverless or container-based deployment.
* A solid grasp of core security concepts (the incident response lifecycle, common vulnerability classes, attack frameworks such as MITRE ATT&CK), or the curiosity and drive to ramp up quickly.
* Clear written and verbal communication in English; able to document systems and decisions for both technical and non-technical audiences.

**Good to have**
----------------

* Experience with SOAR or workflow-automation platforms (Tines, Torq, n8n, or similar).
* Hands-on experience deploying AI agents or LLM-powered applications in production.
* Familiarity with security tooling such as SIEM, EDR, WAF, or vulnerability scanners (Semgrep, SonarQube, Coana, etc.).
* Experience with infrastructure-as-code (Terraform), containers, and CI/CD pipelines.
* Familiarity with compliance frameworks such as ISO 27001, SOC 2, or NIST CSF.
* Relevant certifications — cloud (AWS), security (CompTIA Security+, CySA+), or equivalent; though we consider demonstrated experience over credentials.
* Degree in Computer Science, Information Security, or a related field — though we value demonstrated experience over credentials.

**What it's like to work at Gelato**
------------------------------------


We are a customer-obsessed team with the ambition to change the world by connecting technology to the printing industry and making it much more sustainable. Everyone who joins our team must feel genuinely intrigued and motivated by our mission. We expect a lot. We are a driven team with big goals, so we seek individuals who are genuinely passionate about their work and possess an entrepreneurial spirit. Our culture is unique and we live by our values, so it's worth learning more about our culture and how we work before presenting your application.


At Gelato, we pride ourselves on our global presence with 14 offices worldwide, fostering a dynamic and diverse work environment. Rooted in a culture that values collaboration, creativity, and camaraderie, we actively cultivate a company culture that thrives on shared experiences. We encourage team members to embrace this culture by working from our inspiring office spaces at least three days a week, allowing for meaningful connections and collective growth.


Lastly, we ask that you please upload your CV in English, regardless of which country you are applying from.

title: Platform Engineer
description:
**What will you do?**


As a Platform Engineer you will design, build, and evolve Rebtel's cloud platform and developer infrastructure. You'll play a key role in ensuring our services are reliable, scalable, and secure while enabling engineering teams to deliver faster through modern DevOps practices. Your daily work will span cloud infrastructure, Kubernetes, CI/CD, observability, platform modernization, and close collaboration with engineering teams across the company.

**Areas of ownership:**

* **Design, build, and maintain our cloud infrastructure** across Azure and GCP using Terraform, including networking, IAM, AKS clusters, node pools, autoscaling, and the foundational components that support our services.
* **Build and improve our CI/CD platform** using GitHub Actions and Azure DevOps. Create reusable workflows and automate Docker image builds and deployments.
* **Streamline software delivery** by driving GitOps-based deployments across development, staging, and production environments.
* **Own and evolve our monitoring and observability ecosystem**, including Elastic/Kibana, Datadog, Application Insights, and distributed request tracing. Enable engineers to quickly diagnose and resolve production issues.
* **Optimize our platform for reliability and scale** by improving AKS workloads, Horizontal Pod Autoscalers, RabbitMQ clusters, and SQL Server infrastructure to handle significant traffic spikes during key seasonal events.
* **Implement and maintain secure cloud environments** through Azure Key Vault, IAM/RBAC, network segmentation, and infrastructure best practices.
* **Help ensure the secure handling of payment-related traffic** while continuously strengthening our cloud security posture.
* **Help modernize our platform** by migrating legacy .NET Framework services running on Azure VMs to containerized .NET 10 workloads on AKS.
* **Build on the modernization patterns** already established across our backend services.
* **Collaborate across engineering teams** to solve complex operational challenges, improve platform capabilities, and foster a strong DevOps culture throughout the organization.

**Requirements:**

* You are an excellent communicator and collaborator. We work in English, but you will hear many languages in our Stockholm office
* 5+ years of experience in Platform Engineering, DevOps, Site Reliability Engineering, or a similar role.
* Strong hands-on experience with Azure, including AKS, ACR, Key Vault, networking, and identity management.
* Deep understanding of Kubernetes, container orchestration, Helm, and Infrastructure as Code with Terraform.
* Experience building and maintaining CI/CD pipelines using GitHub Actions and/or Azure DevOps.
* Strong understanding of observability practices, including logging, metrics, tracing, alerting, and production incident management.
* Solid understanding of Linux, Windows Server, networking, and cloud security principles.
* Experience deploying and operating modern .NET applications is considered a strong advantage.
* You have a proactive, ownership-driven mindset and remain calm and effective when solving complex production challenges.

**Why Rebtel?**


Rebtel has been connecting people across borders for nearly 20 years. Today, we’re profitable, growing, and at a pivotal moment in our journey. As we enter our next phase, we’re building an organisation designed for speed, ownership, and real impact, where every role contributes directly to shaping what comes next.


This is a place with global ambition and a strong foundation, where ideas move quickly and decisions matter. You won’t get lost in layers of process or slow-moving structures. Instead, you’ll find the space to take ownership, collaborate across teams, and make meaningful contributions from day one. Based in Stockholm, we bring together a diverse, international team united by a shared purpose: to simplify the way people connect worldwide.


At Rebtel, you are the most important asset and we strive to provide a comprehensive package of benefits and perks that enhance your well-being and work experience. Here are some of the things you can expect from us:

* Pension Plan
* Health Checkups, Influenza shots and Private Medical Insurance
* Dental Insurance
* Occupational insurance
* Wellness allowance (5,000 SEK)
* Discount on gym memberships
* Bonus program
* Extra parental pay
* 30 days annual vacation
* Monday breakfasts
* Relocation Support, if you're joining us from afar, we'll assist you in making a smooth transition.

*We are Rebtel. We come from all around the world to create products for anyone who has crossed a border. We believe in equal opportunity and value diversity at our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status.*
company_description: Our team mirrors our global audience: we are a diverse team of people from over 30 different countries that get together everyday.

title: DevOps Engineer II
description:
**Description & Requirements**
 Electronic Arts creates next-level entertainment experiences that inspire players and fans around the world. Here, everyone is part of the story. Part of a community that connects across the globe. A place where creativity thrives, new perspectives are invited, and ideas matter. A team where everyone makes play happen.
   

  

 EA SPORTS is one of the leading sports entertainment brands in the world, with top-selling videogame franchises, award-winning interactive technology, fan programs, and cross-platform digital experiences. EA SPORTS creates connected experiences that ignite the emotion of sport through industry-leading sports video games, including Madden NFL football, EA SPORTS FC, NHL® hockey, and EA SPORTS UFC.
   

  

 The Tracab team is part of the EA SPORTS Tech organization. TRACAB’s advanced sports optical tracking helps unlock a new level of enhanced realism, animation and dynamic, real-time simulation for our fans, allowing us to generate near real-time insights for media broadcast teams, referees, players, coaches, and fans.
   

  

 We are looking for a DevOps Engineer to join our team at Tracab, where you'll help us create cutting-edge sports broadcasting products. Your work will ensure our solutions are secure, reliable, and continuously improved to meet the high expectations of our customers.
   

  

**Requirements**
* Software and/or release engineering experience
* Ability to understand and work with complex releasing systems
* Algorithmization and scripting ability (Groovy, Python, PowerShell, Bash or any programming language)
* CI/CD software (Preferably Jenkins, GitLab CI, Azure DevOps, GitHub Actions, any other similar automation software)
* Docker experience (including creating images, Docker networking and Docker Compose).
* Strong experience with AWS - IAM,EC2,VPC,S3,ECS,Lambdas
* Using Terraform on daily bases
* Ansible and Packer experience
* Windows and Linux experience - (we are working on both OS)
* Experience with Atlasian softwares (Jira, Confluence, Bitbucket)


**Nice To Have**
* Experience with releasing C++, Java, C#/.NET, or JS/TS/NodeJS applications
* Experience with SQL, noSQL databases, or MQs
* Experience with Kubernetes
* Advanced release concepts experience - releases of SaaS, releases to cloud, feature flagging, canary releases
* Experience with large-scale or distributed systems, SOA


**About Electronic Arts**
 We’re proud to have an extensive portfolio of games and experiences, locations around the world, and opportunities across EA. We value adaptability, resilience, creativity, and curiosity. From leadership that brings out your potential, to creating space for learning and experimenting, we empower you to do great work and pursue opportunities for growth.
   

  

 We adopt a holistic approach to our benefits programs, emphasizing physical, emotional, financial, career, and community wellness to support a balanced life. Our packages are tailored to meet local needs and may include healthcare coverage, mental well-being support, retirement savings, paid time off, family leaves, complimentary games, and more. We nurture environments where our teams can always bring their best to what they do.
   

  

 Electronic Arts is an equal opportunity employer. All employment decisions are made without regard to race, color, national origin, ancestry, sex, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, genetic information, religion, disability, medical condition, pregnancy, marital status, family status, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. We will also consider employment qualified applicants with criminal records in accordance with applicable law. EA also makes workplace accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities as required by applicable law.

title: Platform Engineer
description:
We are hiring for - Platform Engineer
 



 Work location - Stockholm, Sweden (Hybrid)
 



  





  





 Role:
 



 • As a Platform Engineer, you will design and build a cloud based custom Observability Platform, leveraging software-driven approaches to tackle complex, data-intensive challenges.

title: DevSecOps-ingenjör
description:
**Vill du vara med och bidra till ett säkrare samhälle?** 




 Vi utvecklar marknadens säkraste it-plattform/ PaaS, Craton för organisationer med de högsta skyddskraven. Vi hjälper samhällsviktiga aktörer att skydda sina mest värdefulla tillgångar mot både yttre och inre hot. Genom våra lösningar bidrar vi till att hålla i gång de verksamheter som får samhället att fungera även vid stora störningar eller kriser. Idag är vi ett av landets ledande företag inom verksamhets- och säkerhetsskydd och vi växer.
 



 Om du vill vara med på en spännande utvecklingsresa i rollen som DevSecOps-ingenjör där ditt arbete gör skillnad, då är Basalt platsen för dig. Hos oss får du bygga en stark kompetens inom system som ska leva upp till de högsta säkerhetskraven, ett område med växande betydelse för hela samhället.
 



  





**Om rollen** 




 I rollen som DevSecOps-ingenjör på Basalt AB kommer du arbeta med vår plattform Craton, du kan läsa mer om Craton här.
 



  





 Du kommer jobba med att designa, implementera och förvalta säkra utvecklings- och driftsmiljöer baserade på on-prem-infrastruktur. Du fungerar som en teknisk nyckelresurs som driver integrationen av säkerhet i hela mjukvarulivscykeln och bidrar aktivt till omvärldsbevakning samt kunskapsspridning internt och mot kund.
 



  





**Dina huvudsakliga arbetsuppgifter:** 



* Design och implementation av säkra CI/CD-pipelines med automatiserade säkerhetskontroller (SAST, DAST, SCA, secrets scanning).
* Arkitektur och förvaltning av on-prem-infrastruktur med OpenStack och Kubernetes, med säkerhet och skalbarhet i fokus.
* Leda och genomföra hotmodellering, riskanalyser och säkerhetsgranskningar av infrastruktur och applikationer.
* Implementera och förvalta infrastruktur som kod (IaC) med verktyg som Terraform, Pulumi eller Ansible.
* Ansvara för och driva omvärldsbevakning – följa hotlandskapet, analysera nya sårbarheter och trender samt producera rapporter och underlag för interna och externa intressenter.
* Stödja och vägleda kollegor samt bidra till kompetensutveckling inom teamet.
* Driva arbetet med Zero Trust-principer, härdning av miljöer och efterlevnad av relevanta ramverk (t.ex. ISO 27001, NIST, CIS Benchmarks).
* Driva en säker användning av AI i utvecklingsarbetet.



  





**Du ansvarar för att:** 



* Äga och vidareutveckla teamets tekniska processer och verktygskedja inom DevSecOps.
* Säkerställa att säkerhetskrav integreras tidigt i utvecklingsprocessen (shift-left).
* Identifiera, prioritera och driva åtgärdsarbete kring sårbarheter och säkerhetsbrister.
* Sammanställa och kvalitetssäkra omvärldsbevakning till interna rapporter och kundleveranser.
* Dokumentera arkitekturbeslut, säkerhetskrav och driftsprocedurer.



  





**Din bakgrund** 




 För rollen efterfrågas följande kompetens:
 


* Relevant akademisk bakgrund inom datateknik, informationssäkerhet eller motsvarande erfarenhet.
* Minst 5 års erfarenhet av DevOps, DevSecOps eller liknande tekniska roller.
* Erfarenhet av att bygga och driftsätta CI/CD-pipelines i verkliga miljöer (t.ex. GitHub Actions, GitLab CI eller Azure DevOps).
* Kunskap inom containerteknologier och orkestrering, i synnerhet Kubernetes i on-prem-miljö, samt tillhörande säkerhetsaspekter.
* Erfarenhet av OpenStack eller liknande plattformar för on-prem-virtualisering.
* Erfarenhet av omvärldsbevakning och förmåga att analysera och kommunicera hotinformation.
* Erfarenhet av säkerhetsverktyg såsom Trivy, Snyk, SonarQube, Falco eller liknande.



  





**Meriterande** 



* Certifieringar inom området, t.ex. CKS, CISSP eller motsvarande.
* Erfarenhet av att arbeta med drift är meriterande.



  





**Din ansökan** 




 Ansök med ditt CV och beskriv kort vad du söker i din nästa roll. Vi gör löpande urval och intervjuer så ansök gärna redan idag!
 



  





 Har du frågor är du varmt välkommen att höra av dig till hanna.stockelid@basalt.se
 



  





**Placering och säkerhet** 




 Vi har kontor i centrala Stockholm och i Enköping och du utgår från det kontor som passar dig bäst.
 



  





 Vi ställer höga krav på våra medarbetares säkerhets- och sekretessmedvetande. Eftersom våra kunders verksamhet kräver det och flera av våra anställda är placerade i säkerhetsklass, är svenskt medborgarskap ett villkor för att arbeta på Basalt.
 



  





**Vad vi erbjuder** 




 För att ge dig trygghet och stabilitet erbjuder vi ett förmånspaket som inkluderar en av marknadens bästa pensionslösningar, omfattande försäkringar, förstärkt föräldralön och ett generöst friskvårdsbidrag på 5 000 kr/år. Du får även tillgång till Benifex, där du kan hitta extra förmåner och vi erbjuder kontinuerliga möjligheter till kompetensutveckling. Vi erbjuder också tjänstledighet med löneutfyllnad för medarbetare som tjänstgör inom Försvarsmakten, inklusive GSS/T, reservofficerare och hemvärnssoldater.
 



  





 Vi värnar om balansen mellan arbete och fritid. Med flexibla arbetstider, 5-timmars arbetsdag innan helgdagar och ledigt på klämdagar ser vi till att du har tid för både ditt jobb och ditt privatliv. Dessutom anordnar vi gemensamma aktiviteter, såsom skidresor, träningspass samt sociala luncher och frukostar, för att stärka sammanhållningen och göra arbetsplatsen ännu trevligare.
 



  





 Vi strävar efter att du ska känna stolthet och trivsel hos oss på Basalt. Du kan läsa mer om våra förmåner och om hur det är att arbeta på Basalt här!
 



  





**Basalt i korthet:** 



* Grundat 2009, idag ca 150 medarbetare
* Kontor i Stockholm och Enköping
* En del av Omegapoint Group
* Ledande inom säkra it-lösningar och systematiskt säkerhetsarbete
* Värderingar: vass och snäll
* Vision: att vara det självklara valet för ett robust och säkert samhälle



  





 Varmt välkommen med din ansökan!

title: Data Engineering Lead, Founding Team | Stockholm
description:
**Who We Are** 




 Vantir is an AI-native consulting and venture-building firm based in Stockholm. We’re a team of engineers and entrepreneurs helping companies build, scale, and apply modern data and AI capabilities. We work closely with ambitious clients while also building ventures of our own. Now we’re looking for a Data Engineering Lead to join our founding team and help shape both our client work and the company we’re building.
 



  





**Role Description** 




 This is a full-time, on-site role based in Stockholm for a Data Engineering Lead joining Vantir’s founding team. You’ll take a leading role in designing and building modern data platforms, pipelines, and AI-ready infrastructure for our clients. You’ll work hands-on across architecture, implementation, and technical leadership, while also helping define how Vantir delivers world-class data and AI consulting. As part of the founding team, this is not just a delivery role. You’ll help shape our engineering culture, technical standards, ways of working, and the kind of company Vantir becomes.
 



  





**What’s in It for You?** 



* A rare opportunity to join a newly founded consulting and venture-building firm as part of the founding team.
* A leading role in high-impact data and AI projects with ambitious clients.
* The chance to shape Vantir’s technical direction, delivery model, and engineering culture.
* Opportunities to work with our venture-building business and help turn ideas into products.
* A collaborative team where your ideas matter and your work has visible impact.
* Competitive compensation and benefits.



  





**What We’d Love to See in You** 



* Master’s degree in Engineering, Technology, Computer Science, or a related field.
* At least 5 years of experience in data engineering, software engineering, analytics engineering, AI engineering, or similar.
* Strong experience designing and building modern data platforms, data pipelines, and production-grade data systems.
* Experience with cloud platforms such as AWS, GCP, or Azure.
* Strong software engineering fundamentals and experience with modern development practices.
* Experience with tools such as Snowflake, dbt, Databricks, Airflow, Spark, or similar modern data stack technologies.
* Ability to lead technical discussions, make architectural decisions, and guide other engineers.
* A pragmatic, business-oriented mindset and a passion for using data to create real impact.
* You live in or near Stockholm.



  





**Ready to make your mark?** 




 Apply today and help us build one of Sweden’s most exciting data and AI consulting firms from the ground up.
 



 Learn more about the journey at
 **vantir.se/careers** 
 .

title: Client Delivery Manager
description:
Goteborg, Västra Götalands län
  
Job Summary
  
This role is responsible for supporting client delivery. It focuses on maintaining strong client partnerships, ensuring smooth delivery operations, and aligning outcomes with business objectives, while contributing to key transformation and risk management efforts.
  
Key Responsibilities
  
1.Support client delivery programs using digital engineering approaches and enterprise platforms, aligning delivery with client needs and organisational standards.
  
2. Contribute to operational governance by applying delivery frameworks and tracking performance metrics using tools such as ServiceNow, Jira, and SAP.
  
3. Support risk management efforts by applying analytics and established practices to help identify and address delivery risks across business and engineering areas.
  
4. Work with cross-functional teams to coordinate resources and support programme delivery and client satisfaction.
  
5. Contribute to transformation initiatives , leveraging cloud, automation, and emerging technologies to enhance delivery outcomes.
  
6. Build and maintain client relationships, identifying opportunities for growth and solution improvement .
  
7. Support adherence to contractual obligations and industry standards, applying frameworks such as ITIL, PMI, and Agile to ensure consistent delivery
  
  

Other Requirements
  
  

Resource extension, Hanish Prashar, July - Aug.

title: Enterprise Account Executive - Expansion
description:
At Snowflake, we are powering the era of the agentic enterprise. To usher in this new era, we seek AI-native thinkers across every function who are energized by the opportunity to reinvent how they work. You don’t just use tools; you possess an innate curiosity, treating AI as a high-trust collaborator that is core to how you solve problems and accelerate your impact. We look for low-ego individuals who thrive in dynamic and fast-moving environments and move with an experimental mindset — who rapidly test emerging capabilities to discover simpler, more powerful ways to deliver results. At Snowflake, your role isn't just to execute a function, but to help redefine the future of how work gets done.


The Enterprise Account Executive - Insurance is responsible for building client relationships with enterprise businesses within a specified region. People who excel at this job have the ability to prospect, develop, and close business within a timely manner while focusing on the clients’ requirements. The Enterprise AE's must have the confidence and ability to negotiate and close agreements with clients and support new customers through our on-boarding process.  

  

This role is a unique opportunity to contribute in a meaningful way to high visibility, high impact projects at a very exciting time for the company. Snowflake is an innovative, high-growth, customer-focused company in a large and growing market. If you are an energetic, self-managed professional with experience managing a complex sales process and possess excellent presentation and listening skills, organization and contact management capabilities, we’d love to hear from you.

**AS AN ACCOUNT EXECUTIVE AT SNOWFLAKE YOU WILL:**

* Achieve sales quotas for allocated accounts and/or territory on a quarterly and annual basis by developing a sales strategy in the allocated territory with a target prospect list, and a regional sales plan.
* Develop marketing plans with the marketing team to drive revenue growth.
* Be the trusted advisor to the customer by understanding their existing and future IT roadmap to drive the Snowflake solution within the marketplace.
* Prospect qualification and the development of new sales opportunities and ongoing revenue streams.
* Arrange and conduct initial Executive and CxO discussions and positioning meetings.
* Sales process management and opportunity closure.
* Ongoing account management to ensure customer satisfaction and drive additional revenue streams.
* Be familiar with solution-based approach to selling, have experience managing a complex sales process and possess excellent presentation and listening skills, organization and contact management capabilities.

**OUR IDEAL ACCOUNT EXECUTIVE WILL HAVE:**

* 8+ years of field sales experience preferred with an emphasis on data or cloud related technology.
* 5+ year’s experience focussed on generating new business within Enterprise accounts.
* Proven ability to independently manage, develop, and close new client relationships.
* Experience hitting quota of $1M+ of ARR per year and experience on averaging 8+ face to face meetings per week.
* New business sales focus.
* A track record of success in driving consistent activity, pipeline development and quota achievement.
* Experience determining customer requirements and presenting appropriate solutions.
* Pro-active, independent thinker with high energy and a positive attitude.
* Excellent verbal and written communication, presentation, and relationship management skills.
* Ability to thrive in fast-paced startup environment.
* Executive level relationship management.
* Ability to understand the "bigger picture" and the business drivers around IT.
* BA/BS required.

**BONUS POINTS FOR EXPERIENCE WITH THE FOLLOWING:**

* Industry specific experience - Telco & Hospitality are highly advantageous.
* Experience implementing MEDDPICC.
* Experience selling software or cloud based applications to the Enterprise is desired.


Snowflake is growing fast, and we’re scaling our team to help enable and accelerate our growth. We are looking for people who share our values, challenge ordinary thinking, and push the pace of innovation while building a future for themselves and Snowflake.


How do you want to make your impact?


For jobs located in the United States, please visit the job posting on the Snowflake Careers Site for salary and benefits information: careers.snowflake.com
